title: Logged-Out Web Scrape
short_description: Unusual patterns spotted in logged-out web traffic against web.phantomfeed.io. Determine whether automated scraping is occurring, identify the responsible clients, and explain what they are doing.

schema_hints.timestamp: ISO-8601 request time. Useful for rate-per-minute analysis.
schema_hints.uri: Full request URI. Endpoint templates reveal enumeration patterns — walk a numeric ID space?
schema_hints.userID: '0' for logged-out traffic (about 23% of rows). Logged-in user IDs are present too. The interesting activity for this scenario is in the logged-out portion.
schema_hints.IP: Source IP. Non-routable / synthetic for safety. Per-IP volume can mislead — look deeper.
schema_hints.ASN: Autonomous System Number. Compare consumer-ISP ASNs vs datacenter ASNs vs known residential-proxy provider ASNs. The ASN composition of a cluster is often the loudest signal.
schema_hints.requestHeaders: Serialized JSON of headers. Order and presence matters — real browsers send specific headers, scrapers often omit them.
schema_hints.requestCookieNames: Comma-separated cookie name list. Empty cookies on a web page request is a strong tell.
schema_hints.JA4: TLS client fingerprint. Identical JA4 across thousands of IPs is suspicious. But beware — real browsers also share JA4 fingerprints.
schema_hints.userAgent: Browser/app UA string. Spoofable but useful in combination with other signals.
schema_hints.parameters: Query string parameters. Fixed parameters across many IPs = enumeration.
schema_hints.responseContentSize: Response body size in bytes. Consistent sizes suggest template responses (scraping).
schema_hints.authStatus: Either 'logged_out' or 'logged_in'. The interesting activity for this scenario is in the logged-out subset.

hints.label: Look at signals together, not in isolation
hints.text: Single signals (e.g. high request volume per IP) will mislead you here — see the partner and power users. Combine signals: JA4 + missing cookies + ASN type + endpoint pattern.

hints.label: Cookies and referer are powerful
hints.text: Real browser users will almost always send session cookies and a referer chain. A request with no cookies hitting a logged-out web page is unusual.

hints.label: Endpoint enumeration
hints.text: Once you find a suspicious cluster, look at the URI patterns. Is the same endpoint template being walked across many values? That's enumeration.

hints.label: ASN reputation matters
hints.text: Several major commercial residential-proxy providers operate well-known ASNs. Their appearance in your traffic is itself signal. Cross-reference your suspicious IPs against a known-proxy ASN list (publicly maintained ones exist).

hints.label: Distinguish the partner
hints.text: There's a legitimate partner integration with high volume too. They identify themselves via a labeled User-Agent and partner-specific cookies. Make sure your detection doesn't false-positive them.

sample_queries.title: Cookie state distribution
sample_queries.description: Find requests that arrive with no cookies — unusual for browser users.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT requestCookieNames, COUNT(*) AS hits, COUNT(DISTINCT IP) AS unique_ips
FROM phantomfeed_logs_logged_out
GROUP BY requestCookieNames
ORDER BY hits DESC

sample_queries.title: JA4 + no cookies
sample_queries.description: Stack two signals — fingerprint reuse and missing cookies.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT JA4, userAgent, COUNT(*) AS hits, COUNT(DISTINCT IP) AS ips,
       COUNT(DISTINCT ASN) AS asns
FROM phantomfeed_logs_logged_out
WHERE requestCookieNames = ''
GROUP BY JA4, userAgent
ORDER BY hits DESC

sample_queries.title: ASNs of the suspect cluster
sample_queries.description: Which ASNs host the no-cookie traffic? Look up names of unknown ASNs.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT ASN, COUNT(*) AS hits, COUNT(DISTINCT IP) AS ips
FROM phantomfeed_logs_logged_out
WHERE requestCookieNames = ''
GROUP BY ASN
ORDER BY hits DESC

sample_queries.title: What endpoints are being hit
sample_queries.description: What is the scraper after? Group by URI pattern.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN uri LIKE '%/u/user_%' THEN '/u/user_<N>'
    WHEN uri LIKE '%/p/post_%' THEN '/p/post_<N>'
    WHEN uri LIKE '%/groups/group_%' THEN '/groups/group_<N>'
    WHEN uri LIKE '%/api/partner%' THEN 'partner-api'
    ELSE 'other'
  END AS template,
  COUNT(*) AS hits,
  COUNT(DISTINCT IP) AS ips
FROM phantomfeed_logs_logged_out
WHERE requestCookieNames = ''
GROUP BY template
ORDER BY hits DESC

sample_queries.title: Partner traffic (do not false-positive)
sample_queries.description: Compare partner traffic — it has a labeled UA and cookies.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT userAgent, requestCookieNames, IP, ASN, COUNT(*) AS hits
FROM phantomfeed_logs_logged_out
WHERE userAgent LIKE '%Partner%'
GROUP BY userAgent, requestCookieNames, IP, ASN

sample_queries.title: Rate per IP (suspect cluster)
sample_queries.description: Per-IP rates are LOW for distributed scrapers — that's the point of a proxy pool.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT IP, ASN, COUNT(*) AS hits
FROM phantomfeed_logs_logged_out
WHERE requestCookieNames = ''
GROUP BY IP, ASN
ORDER BY hits DESC
LIMIT 30
